response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASURE OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE. THE MOLECULES IN A SUBSTANCE MOVE MORE AGRESSIVELY WHEN THE TEMPERATURE IS HIGHER. WHEN THE TEMPERATURE INCREASES, THE MOLECULES MOVE FASTER AND COLLIDE MORE FREQUENTLY. THIS INCREASE IN COLLISIONS CAN CAUSE PHYSICAL CHANGES IN THE SUBSTANCE.
